title: Bourbon and Brown Sugar Barbecue Sauce
description: Sweet, smoky, and just a little dangerous. The bourbon cuts through the sugar while the molasses brings depth. This is not your average squeeze bottle sauce.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan over medium heat.
Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
Reduce heat and simmer, stirring often, until the sauce thickens and reduces to about 2 cups, about 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before using.
Store covered in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
Bring to room temperature before serving.
notes: Simmer longer if you want a thicker, stickier sauce.
Brush on during the last few minutes of grilling to avoid burning the sugars.
Works great as both a glaze and a dipping sauce.
whyThisHits: It balances sweet, smoke, and heat without leaning too hard in any direction. The bourbon adds depth instead of just being a gimmick.
pairing: Perfect for ribs, chicken, burgers, or anything coming off a grill. Also solid as a dipping sauce for fries or roasted potatoes.
footerLine: Art for Your Mouth. Because boring food is a waste of time.
